name: Center for Threat-Informed Defense Mappings Explorer
owner: Center for Threat-Informed Defense (operated by MITRE Engenuity, a non-profit subsidiary of MITRE)
description: The Center for Threat-Informed Defense (CTID) publishes cross-framework mappings between MITRE ATT&CK techniques and common security control frameworks. This directory mirrors the mappings-explorer dataset: ATT&CK mappings to AWS, Azure, GCP, Microsoft 365, NIST SP 800-53 r5, VERIS, and CVE. Each mapping file is a JSON manifest that links ATT&CK technique IDs to the framework's control IDs / asset names / vendor capabilities. Used by security architects to answer "which controls cover technique X?" and "what's our coverage gap against ATT&CK?"
relationToCloudSecurity: CTID's cloud-platform mappings (AWS, Azure, GCP, M365) are the canonical reference for translating between ATT&CK technique IDs and cloud-platform security service capabilities. Essential for cloud-platform threat-informed defense assessments and gap analyses.
relationToAISecurity: CTID has published some AI-relevant mappings (notably ATLAS technique mappings and AI-specific scenarios) but the primary AI surface is MITRE ATLAS itself. The mappings here are general-security oriented; check the CTID GitHub org for newer AI-specific work.

license.notes: The mappings-explorer repository (and most CTID projects) are published under Apache-2.0. Both the data (JSON) and any derived structured forms are freely redistributable; preserve CTID and MITRE Engenuity attribution and the Apache-2.0 LICENSE.

lifecycle.notes: CTID releases mappings continuously; the snapshot here is dated by filename (e.g., aws-09.21.2021_attack-9.0-enterprise_json.json embeds the ATT&CK version). Re-pull from upstream for current data.

current_extraction.notes: Files are direct JSON downloads from the mappings-explorer repository. Filename convention encodes the source (aws/azure/gcp/m365/nist_800_53/veris/cve), the snapshot date, and the ATT&CK version mapped against.

extraction_history.reason: Direct JSON downloads from CTID mappings-explorer

notes: Most cited datasets for ATT&CK-to-cloud-platform crosswalks. M365 directory contains per-capability mapping files (40+) that are unusually granular — useful for licensing-tier coverage analysis (E3 vs E5).

desired_end_state.notes: Center for Threat-Informed Defense root namespace. The mappings live at github.com/center-for-threat-informed-defense/mappings-explorer as version-tagged datasets.
